title: AI in Software Development
author: The Secret Developer
text:
Member-only story
AI in Software Development
The Miracle, the Menace, and the Missed Pull Request
There’s a funny little dance we all do when a new technology bursts onto the scene.
Some rush in, feet stomping and arms flailing, declaring it a revolution. Others lurk at the edges, arms folded, saying “we don’t use AI here and never will”. It’s as if they’re proudly announcing their allegiance to Blockbuster in 2010.
In software development, AI isn’t just at the gates. There’s more to it than that.
The Gatekeeper
AI hits hard. It’s running the gatehouse, automating the keycard access, and assigning Jira tickets to the guards. If you want a job you’ll need to get through an AI prescreen before anyone even looks at your application.
Every standup I’m in has at least one person who “used ChatGPT to write the PR description” and because the people who are using AI don’t change anything everyone can tell.
We now get code from AI, tests from AI, and even bug reports written by a bot that ironically understands our acceptance criteria better than our product manager. There’s a reason junior devs are struggling to get a foot in the door because they’re being out-interviewed by a language model that doesn’t mind unpaid overtime or performative Agile…
